key_decisions.thai: ลมก็หยุด
key_decisions.rationale: κοπάζω = 'grow tired, cease, abate.' Thai 'หยุด' (stop/cease) — simple and decisive, matching the sudden cessation. 'ลม' (wind) short and stark.

key_decisions.thai: ทะเลก็สงบราบเรียบอย่างยิ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-abstractnouns: γαλήνη = calm. μεγάλη 'great.' Thai 'สงบราบเรียบ' (calm-smooth-even) — 'ราบเรียบ' captures the visual flatness of the calmed sea surface; 'อย่างยิ่ง' (extremely) intensifies. 'ทะเล' (sea) added as subject — Greek has no explicit subject (implied 'there came a great calm'); Thai benefits from the explicit 'ทะเล' (the sea) to close the scene.

translation.notes: THEOLOGICAL CRUX: The perfect passive imperative πεφίμωσο is extraordinary — Jesus addresses the sea as a personal/demonic entity to be muzzled into continued submission. The sea in OT is chaos (Ps 74:13–14; 89:9; Isa 51:9–10) which only YHWH rebukes. Psalms 89:8-9, 107:28-29 ('he stilled the storm to a whisper, and the waves of the sea were hushed') are the intertextual key: Jesus performs what only YHWH does. The disciples' question in v.41 arises from precisely this recognition. The exorcist-language (ἐπιτιμάω, φιμόω) parallels Mark 1:25, where the same verbs silence an unclean spirit — underlining that for Mark, chaos-forces of nature and demons occupy the same spiritual category.
translation.thai_summary: คำว่า ‘เงียบเถิด จงสงบเถิด’ ในภาษากรีกเป็นคำสั่งให้ทะเล ‘ถูกปิดปาก’ — ใช้กับปีศาจในข้อ 1:25 ในพันธสัญญาเดิม ทะเลเป็นสัญลักษณ์ของความวุ่นวายและพลังปีศาจ (สดุดี 74:13-14, 89:9) มีเพียงพระยาห์เวห์เท่านั้นที่ทรงสั่งทะเลได้ — พระเยซูจึงทรงกระทำสิ่งที่สงวนไว้สำหรับพระเจ้าเท่านั้น

greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τί δειλοί ἐστε; οὔπω ἔχετε πίστιν;
bsb_english: "Why are you so afraid?" He asked. "Do you still have no faith?"

translation.thai: แล้วพระองค์ตรัสกับพวกเขาว่า ”ทำไมพวกท่านจึงขลาดกลัวเช่นนี้? ยังไม่มีความเชื่ออีกหรือ?“

key_decisions.thai: ทำไมพวกท่านจึงขลาดกลัวเช่นนี้?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-rquestion: question as rebuke. δειλός = 'cowardly, timid' — stronger than mere φοβέομαι (ordinary fear). Thai 'ขลาดกลัว' (timid-afraid) captures the pejorative shade. 'เช่นนี้' (like-this) adds the reproachful 'so' — 'why are you SO cowardly.' 'พวกท่าน' Jesus-to-disciples respectful-but-firm register.

key_decisions.thai: ยังไม่มีความเชื่ออีกหรือ?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-abstractnouns: πίστις = faith. Per glossary Mark 2:5: 'ความเชื่อ.' 'οὔπω' = 'not yet' — Thai 'ยังไม่...อีก' (not-yet-still). The 'not yet' is crucial: Jesus doesn't say they have no faith, but that they have not YET reached the level of faith that the parables and now this miracle should produce. 'อีกหรือ' (yet?) preserves rhetorical-question form. Jesus could mean: faith in God (uW option 1) or faith in himself (uW option 2) — Thai preserves the ambiguity, as Greek does.

translation.notes: The δειλός/πίστις contrast: cowardice vs. faith. The disciples have SEEN Jesus cast out demons, heal the paralytic, command lepers — yet with a storm they panic. Jesus' rebuke points backward (should have known) and forward (more to learn). Thai 'ขลาดกลัว' preserves the harshness — Jesus is not gentle here.

greek: καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν φόβον μέγαν, καὶ ἔλεγον πρὸς ἀλλήλους· Τίς ἄρα οὗτός ἐστιν ὅτι καὶ ὁ ἄνεμος καὶ ἡ θάλασσα ὑπακούει αὐτῷ;
bsb_english: Overwhelmed with fear, they asked one another, "Who is this, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?"

translation.thai: พวกเขาหวาดกลัวอย่างยิ่ง และพูดกันว่า ”ท่านผู้นี้คือใครกันเล่า? เพราะแม้แต่ลมและทะเลก็ยังเชื่อฟังท่าน“

key_decisions.thai: หวาดกลัวอย่างยิ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom: Hebrew-style cognate accusative 'they feared a great fear' = 'were extremely afraid.' Thai 'หวาดกลัว' (fear-awe) + 'อย่างยิ่ง' (extremely) captures the intensification. Note: this is a DIFFERENT fear than their earlier storm-panic — this is awe-terror at Jesus' power, the kind of fear one has before divine presence (cf. Exod 14:31; Isa 6:5). 'หวาดกลัว' carries more reverential-awe than 'ขลาดกลัว' (v.40's cowardice).

key_decisions.thai: พูดกันว่า
key_decisions.rationale: Imperfect = ongoing discussion. 'พูดกันว่า' (talking-to-each-other-that) — Thai doesn't need the royal register for disciples speaking among themselves.

key_decisions.greek: Τίς ἄρα οὗτός ἐστιν; (rhet. q. — but real Q)
key_decisions.thai: ท่านผู้นี้คือใครกันเล่า?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW v.41: NOT a rhetorical question — a genuine question. The disciples ACTUALLY don't yet understand who Jesus is. ἄρα ('then') marks inferential: 'given what he just did, WHO IS HE?' Thai 'ท่านผู้นี้' (this-honored-person) — 'ท่าน' respectful (disciples speaking ABOUT Jesus, not to him — use of 'ท่าน' keeps the honor). 'คือใครกันเล่า?' (who-is-emphatic?) preserves the incredulous-inquisitive tone. The disciples' Q is Mark's structural Q: the entire gospel drives toward the answer, confirmed at the cross (15:39).

key_decisions.greek: καὶ ὁ ἄνεμος καὶ ἡ θάλασσα ὑπακούει αὐτῷ
key_decisions.thai: แม้แต่ลมและทะเลก็ยังเชื่อฟังท่าน
key_decisions.rationale: 'Even the wind and the sea obey him.' Thai 'แม้แต่' (even) + 'ก็ยัง' (also-still) preserves καί's double emphatic force. 'ท่าน' (honorific) preserved since disciples speak about Jesus with emerging reverence even as they don't fully know him. ὑπακούει = 'obey, hearken to' — note that the same wind/sea are described as ACOUSTIC-agents (they HEAR and OBEY) — picking up the hearing-theology of vv.3, 9, 23 and demonstrating that creation itself hears Jesus' command. Number agreement: Greek neut. pl. subjects can take sg. verb — Thai ignores.

translation.notes: KEY CHRISTOLOGICAL QUESTION: 'Who is this?' is THE Markan question. Echoes: Ps 89:8-9 ('O LORD God of hosts, who is like you?... you still the raging of the sea'), Ps 107:29 ('he stilled the storm to a whisper'), Job 38:8-11 (God sets bounds to the sea). For a first-century Jew, the answer is 'only YHWH commands the sea.' Mark places this question at the climax of the chapter to force the reader toward the same conclusion the centurion will declare at 15:39: 'Truly this man was the Son of God.' The double-fear (phobos at the storm, phobos at the Savior) recalls the double-seeing/double-hearing of v.12: recognition comes through trembling awe, not comfortable knowledge. THEME CLOSURE: Chapter 4 opens with 'listen!' (v.3) and closes with 'the wind and sea OBEY' (ὑπακούει = 'hear-under'). Jesus' word that the disciples struggle to hear is heard-and-obeyed by creation itself. Mark's narrative sermon: if even wind obeys him, so must you.
translation.thai_summary: คำถาม ‘ท่านผู้นี้คือใคร’ คือคำถามสำคัญของมาระโก เสียงสะท้อนจากสดุดี 89:8-9 (‘ข้าแต่องค์พระผู้เป็นเจ้าแห่งโยธา ใครเล่าจะเสมอเหมือนพระองค์... ทรงทำให้ทะเลอันคะนองสงบลง’) สำหรับชาวยิวในยุคนั้น คำตอบเดียวคือ — มีเพียงพระยาห์เวห์เท่านั้นที่ทรงบัญชาทะเลได้
